Cursor MCP

REF-142 · 02/24

Prop drilling blocks the Table component refactor

Props pass through four component layers before reaching the toolbar. Refract found the same pattern in three related files.

Activity

R

Refract created the issue via push analysis on behalf of alex · 2min ago

Integrated platforms

GHGLCUWSVSCLGHGLCUWSVSCLGHGLCUWSVSCL

PIPELINE CENTER

All your repositories, organised in a single place

You can connect your repositories, import your code via GitHub, or create projects manually.

repo overview
issue list
scan history

DASHBOARD

Seamless dashboard to check your results

With Refract dashboard you can check your code quality and recent activity for the month.

P&L-style activity chart
security events calendar

CODE SCANNER

A new way to scan your code, simple and clean

Press any issue to inspect it, with diffs, notes, AST entry points, structural analysis, and more.

code scan detail view

ANALYTICS

Analytics to understand the data behind your code

Automatically generated analytics, filtered by category, to surface the data you need to improve your code quality.

analytics dashboard

MCP INTEGRATIONSoon

Use Refract from your favourite editor

Native MCP support for Cursor, Windsurf, Claude Desktop, Cline, and any MCP-compatible agent.

MCP agent in action

GOVERNSoon

Set team rules. Enforce them automatically.

Create guidelines and policies as code, stored in .refract/guidelines.json, versioned in Git. Enforced on every scan.

policies configuration

Pricing

Simple and fair pricing for every need

Cheapest prices in the space

Free

€0/mo

free forever

See what AI left behind. One repo, manual scan, view issues.

  • ✓Up to 1 connected repository
  • ✓Manual scan / rescan
  • ✓Automatic issue detection
  • ✓Up to 1 guideline set
  • ✓Automatic Analytics
  • ✓Local MCP scan (3/day)
  • ✓CLI: local refract check
  • ✓API keys

The full pipeline. Every push scanned. Unlimited everything.

  • ✓Scan on every push

Every push without Refract is debt.

Structural issues don't fix themselves between sprints. They compound. Refract starts catching them today.
